etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 力(力) (lì) -> to separate with forceful speech -> other
etymology.interpretation: The mouth 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") suggests speech, separating or differentiating entities by naming them. The phonetic 力(力) (lì, "strength; force") contributes the pronunciation. The sense of 'other' arises from the idea of distinct separation or differentiation achieved through naming or specifying a different entity.

examples.chinese: 她想要另外一件外套(她想要另外一件外套)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiǎng yào lìngwài yī jiàn wàitào.

examples.chinese: 我们需要另一个办法(我們需要另一個辦法)。
除了这个问题之外(除了這個問題之外)，还有另外一个要考虑(還有另外一個要考慮)。
examples.pinyin: Chúle zhège wèntí zhīwài，hái yǒu lìngwài yīgè yào kǎolǜ.
examples.english: Besides this issue, there is another one to consider.
